what: Three rules that form the package contract
title: Contributing to surveyframe
text: apply every exported function, without exception. Rule 1. Every exported function takes instrument explicitly unless sole purpose construct part instrument. Functions build sf_item, sf_scale, sf_choices, sf_branch, sf_check objects exceptions. Rule 2. Every reporting function returns structured object first prints second. object useful downstream code. print method convenience, primary output. Rule 3. Every validator fails custom condition class R/conditions.R. Raw stop() calls permitted exported functions. Use rlang::abort() named class sframe_ family.

title: Contributing to surveyframe
text: condition classes defined R/conditions.R. Use appropriate class: sframe_validation_error: instrument structure invalid sframe_import_error: file read parsed sframe_branching_error: branching rule references missing item sframe_quality_warning: data quality issue flagged response review sframe_missing_data_warning: missing data exceeds threshold sframe_scoring_warning: scoring proceed due structural issue

title: Contributing to surveyframe
text: .sframe files UTF-8 JSON SHA-256 integrity hash top-level hash key. hash computed full serialised content hash.value field set empty string. modify JSON schema without updating write_sframe(), read_sframe(), schema version DESCRIPTION.

title: Contributing to surveyframe
text: Use sf_ prefix constructor functions Use snake_case throughout em dashes documentation prose Roxygen descriptions complete sentences Every exported function needs @examples block, even example wrapped \dontrun{}

title: Analysing survey responses
code: demo <- sframe_demo_data() instr <- demo$instrument responses <- demo$responses  dim(responses) #> [1] 120  18

title: Analysing survey responses
text: Response data use instrument item IDs column names. Metadata columns respondent ID, start time, submission time declared explicitly.
code: item_ids <- vapply(instr$items, function(x) x$id, character(1)) head(item_ids) #> [1] "visit_type" "dm_1"       "dm_2"       "dm_3"       "sq_1"       #> [6] "sq_2"  names(responses)[1:8] #> [1] "respondent_id" "submitted_at"  "started_at"    "visit_type"    #> [5] "dm_1"          "dm_2"          "dm_3"          "sq_1"

title: Analysing survey responses
text: Use strict = TRUE response file contain known item metadata columns. Use strict = FALSE file may contain additional survey platform columns retained warning.

title: Analysing survey responses
text: Analysis plans stored instrument. current structure uses method-specific roles. Earlier variables test blocks remain compatible.
code: instr$analysis_plan <- list(   list(     id = "RQ1",     research_question = "Is digital marketing associated with satisfaction?",     family = "association",     method = "correlation_spearman",     roles = list(x = "digital_marketing", y = "satisfaction"),     options = list(alpha = 0.05),     status = "valid_plan",     requires_data = TRUE   ),   list(     id = "RQ2",     research_question = "Do visit types differ in satisfaction?",     family = "group_comparison",     method = "mann_whitney",     roles = list(group = "visit_type", outcome = "satisfaction"),     options = list(alpha = 0.05),     status = "valid_plan",     requires_data = TRUE   ) )  results <- run_analysis_plan(responses, instr) results #> Analysis Results: 2 research question(s) #>  #> RQ 1: Is digital marketing associated with satisfaction? #>   Test: correlation_spearman #>   APA:  r_s(118) = 0.54, p < .001 #>  #> RQ 2: Do visit types differ in satisfaction? #>   Test: mann_whitney #>   APA:  U = 1772, z = -0.06, p = 0.951, r = 0.01

title: Analysing survey responses
text: SurveyStudio can preload instrument responses.
code: launch_studio(   instrument = instr,   responses = responses,   screen = "analysis",   launch.browser = FALSE )

title: Building a survey instrument
text: Choice sets define reusable response options. referenced items choice_set.
code: agree5 <- sf_choices(   id = "agree5",   values = 1:5,   labels = c(     "Strongly disagree",     "Disagree",     "Neither agree nor disagree",     "Agree",     "Strongly agree"   ) )  yes_no <- sf_choices(   id = "yes_no",   values = c("yes", "no"),   labels = c("Yes", "No") )

title: Building a survey instrument
text: Items response variables unless type section_break text_block. Required items marked required = TRUE. Item IDs stable response data columns use IDs.
code: intro <- sf_item(   "intro",   "Study introduction",   type = "section_break",   section_intro = "Please answer the following questions about the service." )  visit_again <- sf_item(   "visit_again",   "Would you use this service again?",   type = "single_choice",   required = TRUE,   choice_set = "yes_no" )  sat_1 <- sf_item(   "sat_1",   "The service met my expectations.",   type = "likert",   required = TRUE,   choice_set = "agree5",   scale_id = "satisfaction" )  sat_2 <- sf_item(   "sat_2",   "The service was easy to use.",   type = "likert",   required = TRUE,   choice_set = "agree5",   scale_id = "satisfaction" )  sat_3 <- sf_item(   "sat_3",   "The service was frustrating to use.",   type = "likert",   required = TRUE,   choice_set = "agree5",   scale_id = "satisfaction",   reverse = TRUE )  age <- sf_item(   "age",   "Age in years",   type = "numeric",   required = FALSE )  comments <- sf_item(   "comments",   "Any other comments?",   type = "textarea",   required = FALSE )  attention <- sf_item(   "attention",   "For quality control, please select Agree.",   type = "single_choice",   required = TRUE,   choice_set = "agree5" )

what: Scales, reverse coding, and minimum valid items
title: Building a survey instrument
text: Scale definitions record item membership, scoring method, reverse-coded items, minimum number valid items required compute score.
code: satisfaction <- sf_scale(   id = "satisfaction",   label = "Satisfaction",   items = c("sat_1", "sat_2", "sat_3"),   method = "mean",   min_valid = 2,   reverse_items = "sat_3" )

title: Building a survey instrument
text: Checks keep quality-control logic instrument definition.
code: attention_check <- sf_check(   id = "attention_agree",   item_id = "attention",   type = "attention",   pass_values = 4,   fail_action = "flag",   label = "Instructional attention check" )

title: Building a survey instrument
text: production workflows, strict validation returns validated instrument stops structured error.
code: instr <- sf_instrument(   title = "Service Feedback Survey",   version = "0.3.0",   description = "Short example instrument for a service feedback study.",   authors = "Research team",   languages = "en",   components = list(     agree5, yes_no,     intro, visit_again, sat_1, sat_2, sat_3, age, comments, attention,     satisfaction, attention_check   ) )  validation <- validate_sframe(instr, strict = FALSE) validation$valid #> [1] TRUE validation$problems #> character(0) instr <- validate_sframe(instr) instr$meta$validated #> [1] TRUE

title: Building a survey instrument
text: .sframe file portable surveyframe instrument file. JSON based, keeps validated metadata, can loaded R, SurveyBuilder, SurveyStudio.
code: path <- tempfile(fileext = ".sframe") write_sframe(instr, path, overwrite = TRUE)  loaded <- read_sframe(path) inherits(loaded, "sframe") #> [1] TRUE loaded$meta$title #> [1] "Service Feedback Survey"

code: launch_builder() launch_builder_demo(open = FALSE)

title: EFA, CFA, CB-SEM, and PLS-SEM syntax generation
text: efa_syntax() creates small R code block estimating exploratory factor solution optional psych package.
code: cat(efa_syntax(   items = c("dm_1", "dm_2", "dm_3", "sq_1", "sq_2", "sq_3"),   nfactors = 2,   extraction = "minres",   rotation = "oblimin" )) #> # EFA syntax generated by surveyframe #> rlang::check_installed("psych", reason = "to estimate an EFA solution") #> efa_items <- data[, c("dm_1", "dm_2", "dm_3", "sq_1", "sq_2", "sq_3")] #> psych::fa(efa_items, nfactors = 2, fm = "minres", rotate = "oblimin")

title: EFA, CFA, CB-SEM, and PLS-SEM syntax generation
text: simplest CFA workflow uses scales already defined instrument. cfa_syntax() remains available backward-compatible wrapper.
code: cat(cfa_lavaan_syntax(instr, ordered = TRUE)) #> # lavaan CFA syntax generated by surveyframe #> # Model: Tourism Services Experience Demo CFA #> # Recommended fitting option: std.lv = TRUE #> # Ordered-item option: pass ordered = c(...) to lavaan::cfa() #> # Fit with lavaan only when lavaan is installed. #>  #> # Digital marketing effectiveness (reflective) #> digital_marketing =~ dm_1 + dm_2 + dm_3 #>  #> # Service quality (reflective) #> service_quality =~ sq_1 + sq_2 + sq_3 #>  #> # Sustainability perception (reflective) #> sustainability =~ sus_1 + sus_2 #>  #> # Tourist satisfaction (reflective) #> satisfaction =~ sat_1 + sat_2 #>  #> # Behavioural intention (reflective) #> behavioural_intention =~ bi_1 + bi_2 cat(cfa_syntax(instr)) #> # lavaan CFA syntax generated by surveyframe #> # Model: Tourism Services Experience Demo CFA #> # Recommended fitting option: std.lv = TRUE #> # Fit with lavaan only when lavaan is installed. #>  #> # Digital marketing effectiveness (reflective) #> digital_marketing =~ dm_1 + dm_2 + dm_3 #>  #> # Service quality (reflective) #> service_quality =~ sq_1 + sq_2 + sq_3 #>  #> # Sustainability perception (reflective) #> sustainability =~ sus_1 + sus_2 #>  #> # Tourist satisfaction (reflective) #> satisfaction =~ sat_1 + sat_2 #>  #> # Behavioural intention (reflective) #> behavioural_intention =~ bi_1 + bi_2

title: EFA, CFA, CB-SEM, and PLS-SEM syntax generation
text: Construct objects record construct ID, label, measurement mode, indicator items. Construct modes include reflective, composite, formative, single_item. Lavaan syntax generation v0.3 intended reflective measurement models. PLS-SEM syntax can use composite-style constructs.
code: dm <- sf_construct(   id = "DM",   label = "Digital marketing",   items = c("dm_1", "dm_2", "dm_3"),   mode = "reflective" )  sq <- sf_construct(   id = "SQ",   label = "Service quality",   items = c("sq_1", "sq_2", "sq_3"),   mode = "reflective" )  sat <- sf_construct(   id = "SAT",   label = "Satisfaction",   items = c("sat_1", "sat_2"),   mode = "reflective" )

title: EFA, CFA, CB-SEM, and PLS-SEM syntax generation
text: sf_model() stores measurement structural paths. sem_lavaan_syntax() generates lavaan syntax without requiring lavaan installed.
code: sem_model <- sf_model(   id = "tourism_sem",   label = "Tourism structural model",   type = "cb_sem",   constructs = list(dm, sq, sat),   paths = list(     sf_path("DM", "SQ", label = "a"),     sf_path("SQ", "SAT", label = "b"),     sf_path("DM", "SAT", label = "c_prime")   ),   indirect = list(     sf_indirect("DM", "SQ", "SAT", label = "indirect_DM_SQ_SAT")   ),   options = list(estimator = "MLR", missing = "fiml", standardised = TRUE) )  validate_model(sem_model, instr)  cat(sem_lavaan_syntax(sem_model, instr)) #> # lavaan CB-SEM syntax generated by surveyframe #> # Model: Tourism structural model #> # Recommended summary option: standardized = TRUE #> # Estimator: MLR #> # Missing data method: fiml #>  #> DM =~ dm_1 + dm_2 + dm_3 #> SQ =~ sq_1 + sq_2 + sq_3 #> SAT =~ sat_1 + sat_2 #>  #> # Structural paths #> SAT ~ b*SQ + c_prime*DM #> SQ ~ a*DM #>  #> # Indirect and total effects #> indirect_DM_SQ_SAT := a*b #> total_DM_SAT := c_prime + indirect_DM_SQ_SAT

title: EFA, CFA, CB-SEM, and PLS-SEM syntax generation
text: PLS-SEM planning, use composite constructs seminr_syntax().
code: pls_model <- sf_model(   id = "tourism_pls",   label = "Tourism PLS model",   type = "pls_sem",   constructs = list(     sf_construct("DM", "Digital marketing", c("dm_1", "dm_2", "dm_3"), mode = "composite"),     sf_construct("SQ", "Service quality", c("sq_1", "sq_2", "sq_3"), mode = "composite"),     sf_construct("SAT", "Satisfaction", c("sat_1", "sat_2"), mode = "composite")   ),   paths = list(     sf_path("DM", "SQ"),     sf_path("SQ", "SAT"),     sf_path("DM", "SAT")   ),   options = list(bootstrap = 5000) )  cat(seminr_syntax(pls_model)) #> # seminr PLS-SEM syntax generated by surveyframe #> rlang::check_installed("seminr", reason = "to fit PLS-SEM models") #> measurement_model <- constructs( #>   composite("DM", multi_items("dm_", 1:3)), #>   composite("SQ", multi_items("sq_", 1:3)), #>   composite("SAT", multi_items("sat_", 1:2)) #> ) #>  #> structural_model <- relationships( #>   paths(from = "DM", to = c("SQ", "SAT")), #>   paths(from = "SQ", to = c("SAT")) #> ) #>  #> pls_model <- estimate_pls( #>   data = data, #>   measurement_model = measurement_model, #>   structural_model = structural_model #> ) #>  #> boot_model <- bootstrap_model( #>   seminr_model = pls_model, #>   nboot = 5000, #>   cores = 1, #>   seed = 123 #> ) #>  #> reliability(pls_model) #> ave(pls_model) #> htmt(pls_model)

title: A complete surveyframe workflow
text: surveyframe supports instrument-centred survey research. survey stored typed sframe object can validate, save, reuse, link response data, score, check data quality, analyse reliability item diagnostics, pass reporting syntax-generation functions. usual workflow : Define load instrument. Validate instrument. Collect import response data. Check response quality. Score scales. Run descriptive, reliability, item diagnostics. Generate syntax EFA, CFA, CB-SEM, PLS-SEM workflows. code: model <- sf_model(   id = "demo_model",   label = "Demo structural model",   type = "cb_sem",   constructs = list(     sf_construct("DM", "Digital marketing", c("dm_1", "dm_2", "dm_3")),     sf_construct("SQ", "Service quality", c("sq_1", "sq_2", "sq_3"))   ),   paths = list(     sf_path("DM", "SQ")   ) )  model_json(model) #> { #>   "id": "demo_model", #>   "label": "Demo structural model", #>   "type": "cb_sem", #>   "engine": "lavaan", #>   "measurement": { #>     "constructs": [ #>       { #>         "id": "DM", #>         "label": "Digital marketing", #>         "mode": "reflective", #>         "items": ["dm_1", "dm_2", "dm_3"], #>         "weights": null #>       }, #>       { #>         "id": "SQ", #>         "label": "Service quality", #>         "mode": "reflective", #>         "items": ["sq_1", "sq_2", "sq_3"], #>         "weights": null #>       } #>     ] #>   }, #>   "structural": { #>     "paths": [ #>       { #>         "from": "DM", #>         "to": "SQ", #>         "label": null #>       } #>     ], #>     "covariances": [], #>     "indirect": [] #>   }, #>   "options": [] #> } cat(sem_lavaan_syntax(model, instr)) #> # lavaan CB-SEM syntax generated by surveyframe #> # Model: Demo structural model #> # Recommended summary option: standardized = TRUE #>  #> DM =~ dm_1 + dm_2 + dm_3 #> SQ =~ sq_1 + sq_2 + sq_3 #>  #> # Structural paths #> SQ ~ DM pls_model <- sf_model(   id = "demo_pls",   label = "Demo PLS model",   type = "pls_sem",   constructs = list(     sf_construct("DM", "Digital marketing", c("dm_1", "dm_2", "dm_3"), mode = "composite"),     sf_construct("SQ", "Service quality", c("sq_1", "sq_2", "sq_3"), mode = "composite")   ),   paths = list(     sf_path("DM", "SQ")   ) )  cat(seminr_syntax(pls_model)) #> # seminr PLS-SEM syntax generated by surveyframe #> rlang::check_installed("seminr", reason = "to fit PLS-SEM models") #> measurement_model <- constructs( #>   composite("DM", multi_items("dm_", 1:3)), #>   composite("SQ", multi_items("sq_", 1:3)) #> ) #>  #> structural_model <- relationships( #>   paths(from = "DM", to = c("SQ")) #> ) #>  #> pls_model <- estimate_pls( #>   data = data, #>   measurement_model = measurement_model, #>   structural_model = structural_model #> ) #>  #> boot_model <- bootstrap_model( #>   seminr_model = pls_model, #>   nboot = 5000, #>   cores = 1, #>   seed = 123 #> ) #>  #> reliability(pls_model) #> ave(pls_model) #> htmt(pls_model)

text: write_sframe() validates instrument writes validated object, including validation flag saved model specifications.
code: library(surveyframe)  agree5 <- sf_choices(   "agree5",   values = 1:5,   labels = c("Strongly disagree", "Disagree", "Neutral", "Agree", "Strongly agree") )  visitor_type_choices <- sf_choices(   "visitor_type",   values = c("first_time", "repeat"),   labels = c("First-time visitor", "Repeat visitor") )  sat_1 <- sf_item("sat_1", "The service was reliable.",   type = "likert", choice_set = "agree5", scale_id = "sat") sat_2 <- sf_item("sat_2", "The service was responsive.",   type = "likert", choice_set = "agree5", scale_id = "sat") sat_3 <- sf_item("sat_3", "I would recommend the service.",   type = "likert", choice_set = "agree5", scale_id = "sat")  visitor_type <- sf_item("visitor_type", "Visitor type", type = "single_choice",   choice_set = "visitor_type") sat <- sf_scale("sat", "Satisfaction", items = c("sat_1", "sat_2", "sat_3"))  instr <- sf_instrument(   "Service Survey",   components = list(     agree5, visitor_type_choices, sat_1, sat_2, sat_3, visitor_type, sat   ) )  instr <- validate_sframe(instr) write_sframe(instr, tempfile(fileext = ".sframe"))

title: surveyframe
text: Analysis plans use role-based variable assignment. Earlier .sframe files using variables test fields remain compatible. Supported method IDs include descriptives, missing data, quality checks, reliability, EFA readiness solutions, CFA/CB-SEM/PLS-SEM syntax, chi-square, Fisher’s exact test, McNemar, Cochran’s Q, t-tests, Mann-Whitney, Wilcoxon, one- two-way ANOVA, ANCOVA, repeated-measures ANOVA, Kruskal- Wallis, Friedman, Pearson/Spearman/Kendall correlations, partial correlations, linear logistic regression, mediation, moderation.
code: instr$analysis_plan <- list(   list(     id = "RQ1",     research_question = "Are the satisfaction items associated?",     family = "association",     method = "correlation_spearman",     roles = list(x = "sat_1", y = "sat_2"),     options = list(alpha = 0.05),     status = "valid_plan",     requires_data = TRUE   ) )  run_analysis_plan(resp, instr)

text: id Character. unique identifier check. item_id Character. id item used check. item must defined separately sf_item() included instrument. type Character. check type. title: Validate an instrument object — validate_sframe
text: following checks performed: Duplicate item IDs Invalid item IDs Duplicate choice-set IDs Duplicate scale IDs Items missing labels Items referencing missing choice_set instrument Items referencing missing scale_id instrument Items marked reverse = TRUE without scale_id Choice sets referenced items present instrument Scale items vectors containing IDs present instrument Branching rules referencing item IDs present instrument Attention checks referencing item IDs present instrument Analysis plan roles referencing missing variables models Model specifications referencing missing indicators constructs

title: Validate an instrument object — validate_sframe
code: # Build a minimal valid instrument and validate it cs    <- sf_choices("ag5", 1:5,            c("Strongly disagree", "Disagree", "Neutral",              "Agree", "Strongly agree")) item  <- sf_item("sat_1", "The service met my expectations.",                  type = "likert", choice_set = "ag5", scale_id = "sat") scale <- sf_scale("sat", "Satisfaction", items = "sat_1") instr <- sf_instrument("Demo Survey", components = list(cs, item, scale))  # Non-strict: returns a list without stopping result <- validate_sframe(instr, strict = FALSE) result$valid #> [1] TRUE result$problems #> character(0)  # Strict: returns instrument invisibly when valid validated <- validate_sframe(instr, strict = TRUE) isTRUE(validated$meta$validated) #> [1] TRUE

what: Analysis planning, survey statistics, and model syntax
title: surveyframe 0.3.0
text: Added role-based analysis-plan structure preserving old variables/test analysis blocks. New plans can store family, method, roles, options, hypotheses, decision_rule, reporting_references, status, requires_data. Added common survey analysis helpers: descriptives_report(), missing_data_report(), assumption_report(), posthoc_report(), validity_report(), sample_size_plan(). Expanded run_analysis_plan() dispatch v0.3 method registry, including descriptives, missing data, sparse-table tests, related-sample tests, Kendall partial correlations, two-way ANOVA, ANCOVA, repeated ANOVA, ordinal multinomial logistic regression, mediation, moderation, model-syntax output. Added model specification layer: sf_construct(), sf_path(), sf_covariance(), sf_indirect(), sf_model(), validate_model(), model_json(), add_model(), efa_solution(), efa_syntax(), cfa_lavaan_syntax(), sem_lavaan_syntax(), seminr_syntax(), model_report_template(). Syntax generation require lavaan seminr. cfa_syntax() remains available backward-compatible wrapper around cfa_lavaan_syntax(). SurveyBuilder Analyse mode now uses three-panel Plan/Run/Report workspace variable metadata badges, role-based variable assignment, method options, output preview, reporting references, table-based model builder. Significance level shown inferential methods.

title: surveyframe 0.3.0
text: Added export_static_survey(). produces single, self-contained HTML file runs survey modern browser without Shiny server internet connection. thirteen item types fully rendered (Likert, single choice, multiple choice, matrix, numeric, text, long text, date, slider, rating, ranking, section break, text block). Branching logic, required-field validation, progress bar, welcome thank-pages handled client-side JavaScript. submission browser downloads per-respondent CSV file. optional endpoint_url argument adds parallel JSON POST serverless endpoint (Google Apps Script, Netlify function, etc.). exported file suitable hosting GitHub Pages, Netlify, static file server, can also shared directly e-mail attachment opened disk. SHA-256 hash written .sframe files write_sframe() SurveyBuilder HTML computed using canonicalisation algorithm, instruments round-trip correctly browser R.

title: surveyframe 0.3.0
text: Added launch_dashboard(). Opens five-panel Shiny dashboard exploring collected response data alongside instrument definition, without modifying either. panels : called without arguments dashboard loads bundled tourism services demo. called user-supplied instrument responses argument, opens metadata-mode showing instrument structure. Added sframe_demo_data(), sframe_input_types_demo_data(), launch_builder_demo(), launch_studio_demo(), launch_dashboard_demo() CRAN-safe examples, training, local GUI testing. Added bundled input-types demo instrument simulated response dataset testing SurveyBuilder, SurveyStudio, dashboard, supported item controls. launch_studio() now accepts preloaded instruments, response data frames, CSV response paths, initial screen selection, host, port, browser control. SurveyStudio reads preloaded values startup.

title: surveyframe 0.3.0
text: run_analysis_plan() now implements four additional tests used SurveyBuilder’s test dropdown: anova_one: One-way ANOVA eta-squared effect size. result significant two groups, Tukey HSD post-hoc output included result object. t_test_pair: Paired-samples t-test Cohen’s d_z. wilcoxon_pair: Wilcoxon signed-rank test r effect size. regression_logistic_binary: Binary logistic regression McFadden R-squared overall model chi-square test. full coefficient table returned interpretation. four runners produce APA-formatted summary string interpretation prompt field guide write-.

R/studio_builder.R contains three fully implemented internal functions (sframe_builder_empty_state, sframe_builder_state_from_instrument, sframe_builder_validate_draft) used SurveyStudio startup draft validation. SHA-256 hashing SurveyBuilder HTML includes pure-JavaScript fallback environments crypto.subtle unavailable file:// origins, including common Firefox file:// configurations. Saving .sframe file builder now always succeeds. SurveyBuilder’s rqSuggest box now appears icon plain-language recommendation two variables selected RQ modal. undo redo buttons SurveyBuilder topbar now correctly disabled respective history stacks empty.

title: surveyframe 0.3.0

Internal HTML report generation now applies escaping consistently report titles, instrument metadata, citations, effect labels. Quarto report rendering now cleans temporary render directories RDS files .exit() even rendering fails.
